_comment: Toyota Ventures (VC Associate, Frontier Fund — deep tech / climate) cut. Mirrors their mandate: early-stage sourcing & screening across AI, advanced materials, automation & robotics, and space tech; networker plugged into the deep-tech ecosystem; cap tables + financial-statement fluency. Leads with the hard-tech thesis + 100 founder interviews. Built by /tailor-resume from boozallen base. Never fabricate.
name: Adam Breaux
contact: 713.302.2020  |  abreaux015@gmail.com  |  linkedin.com/in/adambreaux  |  Tulsa, OK (remote; open to SF Bay Area travel)
summary: Investor-operator with a contrarian conviction that the next decade of returns is in deep tech — physical AI, robotics, advanced materials, space, and U.S. reindustrialization — not another AI SaaS app. Built that thesis from the ground up across ~100 founder and operator interviews. Three years across the venture lifecycle: sourced and screened deals for a $100M+ foundation VC arm, built four companies from 0 to 1, and ran financial modeling, cap tables, and LP reporting. Sharp diligence judgment plus rare fluency building production AI systems — including a multi-agent deal-sourcing engine.

items.title: Associate — Chief of Staff to CEO / Venture Operations Lead
bullets: 0-to-1 venture lead: Took Agentech from a blank page to a funded company (~$25M valuation today) — thesis through pivot to AI-powered insurance-claims servicing; built the team and ran sales to the first $600K ARR. Now its part-time CFO and Chief of Staff.

Sourcing & screening: Across the studio's pipeline, sourced and screened early-stage opportunities, ran discovery and commercial-viability assessments, and helped create four companies — direct venture lead on two.

Fundraising, owned end to end: Ran Agentech's $3M seed — investor memo, financial model, cap table, projections, and data room — and coordinated legal and accounting to a fast, fully-subscribed close. Ran the same process across multiple ventures (~$10M raised studio-wide).

Financial modeling & analysis: Built three-statement, returns, and cap-table models plus competitive analysis across 500+ companies; computed fund TVPI for fundraising; stood up LP reporting on an $8M fund and became the direct point of contact LPs come to for updates.

Production AI systems: Built a multi-agent investor-sourcing engine with a continual-learning loop (ranked by geography, thesis, and stage; enriched via Clay) that produced a pipeline of hundreds of investors, plus a Claude-based operations platform spanning CRM, call transcripts, email, calendar, and contracts that auto-drafts briefs, meeting agendas, and proposals.

items.title: Venture Fellow / Investment Diligence
items.org: Atento Capital  —  VC arm of the George Kaiser Family Foundation ($100M+ fund)
bullets: Sourced 14 of 20 finalists and 4 of 7 startups selected to pitch — the most of any of ~12 fellows; screened hundreds of companies.
Ran screening calls, diligenced founding teams, assessed commercial viability, and presented investment theses to internal committees.
Recalled by principals to diligence two Core Fund I deals: bottom-up market sizing, competitive analysis, and financial-projection review delivered directly to a GP.
Judgment, validated: On all three deals I diligenced, I recommended against — on scalability, market size, and an unconvincing expansion story; all three are no longer operating.

items.title: Self-Directed Deep-Tech Investment Research
bullets: Conducted ~100 founder and operator interviews across energy, manufacturing, and industrials to build a deep-tech investment thesis and map emerging trends and opportunities — building exactly the kind of deep-tech network and judgment early-stage sourcing rewards.

Thesis: AI application software is increasingly un-investable — thin moats, incumbent and fast-follower risk, crowded winners; durable returns lie in physical AI, automation and robotics, advanced materials, space, and U.S. reindustrialization — onshoring the capacity to spin up new physical products at speed and scale.
Fluent in SBIR/STTR non-dilutive funding processes; engaged with OCAST and Oklahoma Catalyst, which administer those programs.

items.title: Founding President — starTUp (University of Tulsa VC Club)
bullets: Founded TU's first VC club and placed members as analysts on the 46VC Hurricane Venture Fund, a multi-million-dollar student/alumni fund.

items.title: Chair, Macroeconomic Committee — TU Student Investment Fund ($8M)
bullets: Implemented an ETF strategy and decision process still in use today; made buy/sell calls on macro trends and led analysts to execute.

items.title: Master of Business Administration — Finance & Entrepreneurship
B.S. Exercise Science / Pre-Med
